context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct JavaScript SDK packages, import paths, camelCase API namespaces, client-side Conversation class, signed URL authentication flow, and client tool registration pattern.

checklist.name: Correct server SDK package
checklist.description: server.js imports from '@elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js', NOT from the deprecated 'elevenlabs' package

checklist.name: ElevenLabsClient import
checklist.description: server.js imports ElevenLabsClient (not a different name) from the SDK package

checklist.name: CamelCase agent namespace
checklist.description: server.js uses client.conversationalAi.agents (camelCase 'conversationalAi', not snake_case 'conversational_ai') for agent operations

checklist.name: Conversation import source
checklist.description: client.js imports Conversation from '@elevenlabs/client' (the browser client package), not from '@elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js'

checklist.name: Signed URL generation
checklist.description: server.js uses conversationalAi.conversations.getSignedUrl (or equivalent REST call) to generate a signed URL for the agent

checklist.name: Client startSession
checklist.description: client.js calls Conversation.startSession() to initiate the conversation

checklist.name: SignedUrl in session
checklist.description: client.js passes signedUrl (or signed_url) parameter to startSession, not just an agentId, reflecting the authenticated flow

checklist.name: ClientTools registration
checklist.description: client.js registers client tools via a clientTools object inside Conversation.startSession (with async handler functions)

checklist.name: Event callbacks
checklist.description: client.js includes at least two of: onMessage, onUserTranscript, or onError callbacks in the startSession configuration

checklist.name: Client tool type in agent
checklist.description: server.js defines the tools with type 'client' (not 'webhook' or 'system') in the agent creation for the browser-side tools

checklist.name: Tool return values
checklist.description: At least one client tool handler in client.js returns a value/object (not void/undefined) so the agent can use the result in conversation
